volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 5.4.952 - Section 5.6.1017b)

sections.label: Section 5.4.952
sections.translation: I didn ’ t know that .

sections.label: Section 5.4.952b
sections.original: Atqui sic inventa est : eam iste vitiavit miser .
sections.translation: But so she has been discovered to be ; he , unfortunate youth , has ravished her . When the brother came to know of this being done , in a most towering rage , he —

sections.label: Section 5.4.954
sections.translation: Did what , pray ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.954b
sections.translation: First , bound him in a shocking manner .

sections.label: Section 5.4.955
sections.translation: Bound him ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.955b
sections.original: Atque equidem orante ut ne id faceret Thaide .
sections.translation: And even though Thais entreated him that he wouldn ’ t do so —

sections.label: Section 5.4.956
sections.translation: What is it you tell me ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.956b
sections.original: Nunc minatur porro sese id quod moechis solet ;
sections.translation: Now he is threatening that he will also do that which is usually done to ravishers ; a thing that I never saw done , nor wish to .

sections.label: Section 5.4.957b
sections.translation: With what assurance does he dare perpetrate a crime so heinous ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.958b
sections.translation: How so heinous ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.958c
sections.original: An non tibi hoc maximum est ?
sections.translation: Is it not most heinous ? Who ever saw any one taken up as a ravisher in a courtesan ’ s house ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.960b
sections.translation: I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 5.4.960c
sections.translation: But that you mayn ’ t be ignorant of this , Pythias , I tell you , and give you notice that he is my master ’ s son .

sections.label: Section 5.4.961b
sections.translation: How ! Prithee , is it he ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.962b
sections.original: Ne quam in illum Thais vim fieri sinat .
sections.translation: Don ’ t let Thais suffer any violence to be done to him . But why don ’ t I go in myself ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.963b
sections.translation: Take care , Parmeno , what you are about , lest you both do him no good and come to harm yourself ; for it is their notion ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.965
sections.original: Quicquid factum est a te esse ortum .
sections.translation: that whatever has happened , has originated in you .

sections.label: Section 5.4.965b
sections.translation: What then , wretch that I am , shall I do , or how resolve ? But look , I see the old gentleman returning from the country ; shall I tell him or shall I not ? By my troth , I will tell him ; although I am certain that a heavy punishment is in readiness for me ; but it ’ s a matter of necessity , in order that he may rescue him .

sections.label: Section 5.4.968b
sections.translation: You are wise . I ’ m going in-doors ; do you relate to him every thing exactly as it happened .

sections.label: Section 5.5.970
sections.original: Ex meo propinquo rure hoc capio commodi ,
sections.translation: I have this advantage [*] from my country-house being so near at hand ; no weariness , either of country or of town , ever takes possession of me ; when satiety begins to come on , I change my locality . But is not that our Parmeno ? Surely it is he . Whom are you waiting for , Parmeno , before the door here ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.975
sections.original: Quis homo est ? ehem , salvum te advenire , here , gaudeo .
sections.translation: Who is it ( Turning round .) Oh , I ’ m glad that you have returned safe .

sections.label: Section 5.5.976
sections.translation: Whom are you waiting for ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.976b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone : my tongue cleaves to my mouth through fright .

sections.label: Section 5.5.977
sections.original: Hem , quid est ? quid trepidas ? satin salve ? dic mihi .
sections.translation: Why , what is it you are trembling about ? Is all quite right ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.5.978
sections.original: Here , primum te arbitrari quod res est velim ;
sections.translation: Master , in the first place , I would have you persuaded of what is the fact ; whatever has happened in this affair has happened through no fault of mine .

sections.label: Section 5.5.980
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.980b
sections.translation: Really you have reason to ask . I ought first to have told you the circumstances . Phaedria purchased a certain Eunuch , to make a present of to this woman here .

sections.label: Section 5.5.982b
sections.translation: To what woman ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.982c
sections.translation: To Thais .

sections.label: Section 5.5.983
sections.translation: Bought ? Good heavens , I ’ m undone ! For how much ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.983b
sections.translation: Twenty minae .

sections.label: Section 5.5.984
sections.translation: Done for , quite .

sections.label: Section 5.5.984b
sections.original: Tum quandam fidicinam amat hic Chaerea .
sections.translation: Then , Chaerea is in love with a certain music-girl here .

sections.label: Section 5.5.985
sections.original: Hem , quid ? amat ? an scit iam ille quid meretrix siet ?
sections.translation: How ! What ? In love ? Does he know already what a courtesan means ? Is he come to town ? One misfortune close upon another .

sections.label: Section 5.5.987
sections.original: Here , ne me spectes : me impulsore haec non facit .
sections.translation: Master , don ’ t look so at me ; he didn ’ t do these things by my encouragement .

sections.label: Section 5.5.988
sections.original: Omitte de te dicere : ego te , furcifer ,
sections.translation: Leave off talking about yourself . If I live , you hang-dog , I ’ ll — But first give me an account of it , whatever it is .

sections.label: Section 5.5.990
sections.original: Is pro illo eunucho ad Thaidem deductus est .
sections.translation: He was taken to the house of Thais in place of the Eunuch .

sections.label: Section 5.5.991
sections.translation: In place of the Eunuch ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.991b
sections.original: Sic est : hunc pro moecho postea
sections.translation: Such is the fact . They have since apprehended him in the house as a ravisher , and bound him .

sections.label: Section 5.5.992b
sections.translation: Death !

sections.label: Section 5.5.993
sections.translation: Mark the assurance of courtesans .

sections.label: Section 5.5.993b
sections.translation: Is there any other calamity or misfortune besides , that you have not told me of ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.995b
sections.translation: That ’ s all .

sections.label: Section 5.5.995c
sections.translation: Do I delay rushing in here ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.996
sections.original: Non dubium est quin mihi magnum ex hac re sit malum ;
sections.translation: There ’ s no doubt but that I shall have a heavy punishment for this affair , only that I was obliged to act thus . I ’ m glad of this , that some mischief will befall these women here through my agency , for the old man has , for a long time , been on the look-out for some occasion [*]

sections.label: Section 5.5.1000
sections.original: Quamobrem insigne aliquid faceret iis . Nunc repperit .
sections.translation: to do them a bad turn ; at last he has found it .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1001
sections.original: Numquam edepol quicquam iam diu quod magis vellem evenire
sections.translation: Never , upon my faith , for a long time past , has any thing happened to me that I could have better liked to happen , than the old gentleman just now , full of his mistake , coming into our house . I had the joke all to myself , as I knew [*] what it was he feared .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1004
sections.translation: Why , what ’ s all this ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1004b
sections.original: Nunc id prodeo ut conveniam Parmenonem .
sections.translation: Now I ’ m come out to meet with Parmeno .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1005
sections.translation: But , prithee , where is he ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1005b
sections.translation: She ’ s looking for me .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1005c
sections.translation: And there he is , I see ; I ’ ll go up to him .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1006
sections.original: Quid est inepta ? quid tibi vis ? quid rides ? pergin ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter , simpleton ? What do you mean ? What are you laughing about ? Still going on ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1006b
sections.translation: I ’ m dying ; I ’ m wretchedly tired with laughing at you .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1007b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1007c
sections.translation: Do you ask ? Upon my faith , I never did see , nor shall see , a more silly fellow . Oh dear , I can not well express what amusement you ’ ve afforded in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1010
sections.original: At etiam primo callidum et disertum credidi hominem .
sections.translation: And still I formerly took you to be a clever and shrewd person . Why , was there any need for you instantly to believe what I told you ? Or were you not content with the crime , which by your advice the young man had been guilty of , without betraying the poor fellow to his father as well ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1014
sections.original: Nam quid illi credis animi tum fuisse ubi vestem vidit
sections.translation: Why , what do you suppose his feelings must have been at the moment when his father saw him clothed in that dress ? Well , do you now understand that you are done for ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1016
sections.original: Hem . Quid dixti , pessima ? an mentita es ? etiam rides ?
sections.translation: Hah ! what is it you say , you hussy ? Have you been telling me lies ? What , laughing still ? Does it appear so delightful to you , you jade , to be making fools of us ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1017b
sections.translation: Very much so .
